name: GT-funcalc
source: Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:503-532 (Banach-algebra function calculus) and :642 (joint continuity of multiplication). For a unital Banach algebra calB, f(x)=sum a_n (x-x0)^n with radius rho, and X in calB with ||X-x0*1||<rho: f(X)=sum a_n (X-x0*1)^n converges; ||f(X)-f(x0*1)|| <= sum_{n>=1}|a_n| ||X-x0*1||^n; the calculus is multiplicative f(X)g(X)=(fg)(X); multiplication is jointly continuous. Instantiated here at calB=End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2), f(x)=x^(-1/2), x0=1, rho=1.
